% Feature fixture: didactic's shared-counter, section-based theorem numbering.
%
% didactic opts into \TeXLibTheorems[numbering=section]{...}, so EVERY family
% (theorem/definition/lemma/...) shares ONE master counter that resets each
% \section and prints as <section>.<n>. The canonical template only proves the
% word "Theorem" renders; this pins the actual sequence, which a counter
% regression (per-family counters, a missing [section] reset, a flat scheme)
% would silently break while the pixel refs still pass. Asserted by
% EXPECT_TEXT["tests/fixtures/Notes"] in smoke_test.py.
%
% No \maketitle / unit-number, so the section counter starts at 0 and the first
% \section is 1 (a unit-number would offset it — out of scope here).
\documentclass{didactic}

\begin{document}

\section{First Section}
\begin{theorem}    Shared counter, entry 1.  \end{theorem}   % Theorem 1.1
\begin{definition} Shared counter, entry 2.  \end{definition}% Definition 1.2
\begin{lemma}      Shared counter, entry 3.  \end{lemma}     % Lemma 1.3

\section{Second Section}
\begin{theorem}    Counter reset for a new section. \end{theorem}   % Theorem 2.1
\begin{definition} Second in section two.          \end{definition}% Definition 2.2

\end{document}
